name: Italian Game
description: Classic open game focused on rapid development, central control, and quick castling.

lines.explanation: The Giuoco Piano develops naturally and prepares a stable center.
notes: Develop quickly
Fight for the center
Castle early
Keep an eye on the f7 square

lines.explanation: Black develops the kingside knight immediately; White can stay calm and build a solid center.
notes: Do not rush tactics when simple development is enough
d3 supports the center and frees the dark-squared bishop

lines.explanation: Black wastes a tempo. White should strike in the center and continue developing.
notes: Punish slow pawn moves with central expansion
Do not hunt cheap tricks

lines.explanation: Black weakens the diagonal and the king. White can use the centralized knight jump tactically.
notes: A weakening move can create immediate tactical chances
Look for forks and king safety problems

lines.explanation: Black jumps forward too early. White can answer with a tactical centralization.
notes: When a piece moves twice early, check for tactical punishments
Central knights often create threats on f7 and c6

name: Ruy Lopez
description: A classical opening that pressures the knight on c6 and the center behind it.

lines.explanation: The Morphy Defense is the most famous main line structure of the Ruy Lopez.
notes: Bb5 increases pressure on e5 indirectly
Retreat the bishop while preserving long-term pressure

lines.explanation: The Berlin develops a piece and challenges White's central intentions immediately.
notes: The position stays classical even without ...a6
Stay focused on development and central stability

lines.explanation: Black asks the bishop early but neglects development. White should hit the center.
notes: Do not overreact to harmless pawn moves
Central expansion is often the best punishment

name: Queen's Gambit
description: A fundamental queen pawn opening based on c4 pressure and central tension.

lines.explanation: The Queen's Gambit Declined keeps the center tense and develops flexibly.
notes: c4 challenges Black's center
Nc3 increases pressure on d5 and e4

lines.explanation: The Slav supports d5 before Black commits the bishop.
notes: White can continue normal development
Do not assume every queen pawn structure is the same

lines.explanation: Black plays a slow move. White should continue development and maintain central pressure.
notes: When the opponent loses time, improve your pieces
Do not force matters without a reason
